title: Session Handoff
description: Machine-readable continuation artifact for a single bounded sprint that spans multiple Claude Code sessions. The current sprint's live handoff lives at automation/session_handoff/next_session.json; one file per sprint. This is NOT an autonomous roadmap — every handoff is scoped to a pre-planned sprint and ends when that sprint ends.

schema_version.description: Bumped only on breaking schema changes. Validators must refuse unknown versions.

sprint_id.description: Stable ID for the sprint this handoff belongs to. Example: S-061-session-handoff.

branch.description: Git branch the next session must check out to continue. Usually the same branch this file is committed on.

created_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp the handoff file was written.

handoff_reason.description: Why the current session stopped at this checkpoint.

handoff_reason_note.description: Optional free-text elaboration on handoff_reason. Required when handoff_reason is 'other'.

checkpoint_summary.description: 1-3 paragraph plain-language summary of where the sprint stands at this checkpoint.

completed_items.description: Concrete units of work finished during the originating session(s).

open_items.description: Sprint work that is still outstanding but not blocked.

next_actions.description: Ordered list of the very next concrete steps the resuming session should take.

blocked_items.description: Items that cannot proceed without external input. Each item names what is needed.

files_to_review.description: Repo paths the resuming session should read first.

commands_to_run.description: Optional shell commands the resuming session should re-run to re-establish state. No secrets, no destructive defaults.

tests_required.description: Tests that must pass before this sprint is considered done. Pytest node-ids or shell commands.

guardrails.description: Things the resuming session must NOT do. Encoded as plain-language imperatives.

continuation_prompt.description: Self-contained briefing the resuming session should be primed with. Should reference this file and the sprint plan, not duplicate them.

ready_for_continue.description: False if the artifact is still being assembled; the continue-work workflow refuses to run a handoff with ready_for_continue=false.

sprint_plan_ref.description: Optional repo path or URL pointing at the authoritative sprint plan (e.g. docs/sprint-plans/...).

previous_handoff_ref.description: Optional repo path or commit SHA of the prior handoff in this sprint, for chain-of-custody.

history.description: Append-only audit trail. Each entry records a continue-work workflow run.
